title: Orthochilus montis-elgonis
intro: Orthochilus montis-elgonis, a species of orchid, is a rare and endemic plant found in the Elgon Mountains of Eastern Africa. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Orthochilus montis-elgonis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat fever, cough, and digestive issues.
